title: Same-Lap Set Model For Field Interaction
summary: A pit decision is not only a single-driver state; it depends on the unordered set of competitors sharing the lap, because traffic, cover-stops, and open-air pockets matter.
rationale: A pit decision is not only a single-driver state; it depends on the unordered set of competitors sharing the lap, because traffic, cover-stops, and open-air pockets matter. Set-based models are designed for permutation-invariant group structure and fit the 'all cars on this lap' problem more naturally than pointwise trees.
implementation_hint: Build same-lap sets keyed by `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` with per-car features and a target for each car. Use a tiny Deep Sets or Set Transformer sidecar to output per-car pit propensity from the whole field state, then blend only its OOF predictions with the base tabular model. Keep it narrow and regularized; this is a sidecar, not a replacement.
expected_effect: May recover interaction signal that handcrafted field-relative summaries miss, especially on cover-stop and clean-air cases.
risk: Higher implementation complexity and easier to overfit on only ~20 cars per set; gains may be concentrated in a small subset of races.
